perturbation.original_text: Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew for successive period, 12 months, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Thereafter, the Maintenance Term shall automatically renew for successive periods, 24 months, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement unless Bank of America terminates Maintenance Services pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the automatic renewal period to 24 months could contradict state laws that limit the duration of automatic renewal clauses in service contracts, especially if adequate notice isn't provided.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Business and Professions Code Section 17602
perturbation.law_explanation: California law requires clear and conspicuous disclosure of automatic renewal terms and a simple method for cancellation.  An excessively long renewal period (24 months), combined with inadequate disclosure, might violate this law. Specifically, Section 17602 mandates clear and conspicuous presentation of automatic renewal terms before the consumer pays for the product or service.
perturbation.location: SCHEDULE A Product License Schedule FORM

GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 3. REPRESENTATIONS TO THE PUBLIC [17500 - 17943] ( Part 3 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 63. ) CHAPTER 1. Advertising [17500 - 17606] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 63. ) ARTICLE 9. Automatic Purchase Renewals [17600 - 17606] ( Article 9 added by Stats. 2009, Ch. 350, Sec. 1. ) 17602. (a) It is unlawful for any business that makes an automatic renewal offer or continuous service offer to a consumer in this state to do any of the following: (1) Fail to present the automatic renewal offer terms or continuous service offer terms in a clear and conspicuous manner before the subscription or purchasing agreement is fulfilled and in visual proximity, or, in the case of an offer conveyed by voice, in temporal proximity, to the request for consent to the offer. If the offer also includes a free gift or trial, the offer shall include a clear and conspicuous explanation of the price that will be charged after the trial ends or the manner in which the subscription or purchasing agreement pricing will change upon conclusion of the trial. (2) Charge the consumer’s credit or debit card, or the consumer’s account with a third party, for an
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the automatic renewal period from 12 to 24 months. The Law Explanation states that California law requires clear disclosure of automatic renewal terms and a simple cancellation method, and that a longer renewal period might violate this law. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing BPC 17602(a), states it is unlawful for a business to fail to present the automatic renewal offer terms in a clear and conspicuous manner. Since the changed text increases the renewal period, potentially making it less conspicuous and harder to cancel, it contradicts the law by increasing the risk of violating BPC 17602(a).

perturbation.original_text: WARRANTY PERIOD DURATION Delivery Date: TBD Installation Date: TBD The period commencing on the Installation Date and continuing for the number of days specified: 120 days
perturbation.changed_text: WARRANTY PERIOD DURATION Delivery Date: TBD Installation Date: TBD The period commencing on the Installation Date and continuing for the number of days specified: 15 days
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the warranty period to 15 days may be unreasonably short, potentially conflicting with implied warranty laws under the Uniform Commercial Code (UCC), which many states have adopted.  Such a short warranty might be deemed unconscionable, especially for complex software.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code - Section 2-309. Absence of Specific Time Provisions; Notice of Termination.
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC 2-309 implies a reasonable time if the contract does not specify duration. A 15-day warranty for complex software could be deemed unreasonable, especially if the buyer needs more time to test and identify defects. This modification could render the warranty clause unenforceable.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-309. Absence of Specific Time Provisions; Notice of Termination. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-309. Absence of Specific Time Provisions; Notice of Termination. (1) The time for shipment or delivery or any other action under a contract if not provided in this Article or agreed upon shall be a reasonable time. (2) Where the contract provides for successive performances but is indefinite in duration it is valid for a reasonable time but unless otherwise agreed may be terminated at any time by either party. (3) Termination of a contract by one party except on the happening of an agreed event requires that reasonable notification be received by the other party and an agreement dispensing with notification is invalid if its operation would be unconscionable. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the warranty period from 120 days to 15 days. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation suggest this could be unreasonably short, conflicting with implied warranty laws under UCC. UCC 2-309 (Snippet 1) states that if a contract doesn't specify duration, a 'reasonable time' applies. Reducing the warranty to 15 days for complex software likely contradicts the UCC's requirement for a 'reasonable time', creating a legal contradiction based on the 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: If Bank of America terminates the Maintenance Services, Bank of America shall have the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services without paying any reinstatement fee.
perturbation.changed_text: If Bank of America terminates the Maintenance Services, Bank of America shall have the right to reinstate the Maintenance Services within 5 days, but will be subject to a reinstatement fee equivalent to 50% of the original maintenance fee.
perturbation.explanation: Limiting the reinstatement period to 5 days and adding a 50% reinstatement fee could be seen as an unreasonable restriction that may conflict with contract law principles of good faith and fair dealing. It also potentially clashes with laws prohibiting unreasonable penalties for contract breaches.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 205 states that 'Every contract imposes upon each party a duty of good faith and fair dealing in its performance and its enforcement.' Limiting reinstatement to 5 days with a large fee could be seen as a violation of good faith, particularly if the original agreement didn't specify these conditions, potentially rendering the modified clause unenforceable.
perturbation.location: SCHEDULE A Product License Schedule FORM
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed reinstatement without a fee, but the changed text introduces a reinstatement fee and time limit. The Law Explanation cites Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 205, stating that 'Every contract imposes upon each party a duty of good faith and fair dealing.' Imposing a fee and time limit where none existed before could be interpreted as a violation of this duty, particularly if the original agreement didn't specify these conditions. The changed text introduces new conditions that could be deemed unreasonable restrictions on the right to reinstate, contradicting the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction' definition, where the changed text introduces requirements that violate general contract law principles.
